method_atom: A mean-field solution, carried out on an infinite tree (Bethe lattice) with wired boundary conditions, of a random combinatorial optimization problem in which one selects the minimum-total-cost spanning structure over independent quenched random edge weights. The global optimum is built by a greedy accretion procedure that is placed in exact correspondence with a connectivity/percolation process, so the geometry of the resulting connected clusters can be characterized by a fractal dimension and by a critical dimension that separates a regime with many large clusters from one with only order-one, sharpened by a scaling / epsilon-expansion argument about the upper critical dimension.
object_atom: The random minimum spanning tree of a weighted graph whose edge costs are independent quenched random variables, and the equivalent strongly-disordered spin-glass model of Newman and Stein, whose number of ground states in d-dimensional Euclidean space is set by the count of large connected components of that tree.
brief_reason: What transfers is the tree/mean-field solution of a random optimization problem via a greedy-to-percolation mapping yielding fractal and critical dimensions; what is domain-specific is the minimum spanning tree and its dual strongly-disordered spin-glass ground-state structure.
